name: Catalonia (Spain)

colors.symbolism: the gold of the Crown of Aragon, recalling Wilfred the Hairy's shield
the four palles, by tradition drawn in blood

description: A yellow field charged with four equal horizontal red stripes (palles), making nine bands total alternating yellow and red.
history: The Senyera is among the oldest surviving flag designs in Europe, documented as the arms of the Counts of Barcelona since the late 12th century. It was confirmed as the flag of the autonomous community of Catalonia by the 1979 Statute of Autonomy.

features.symbolism: The four red palles (bars) on gold are the historic arms of the Counts of Barcelona and the Crown of Aragon; tradition holds that King Charles the Bald drew them with his bloodied fingers across Wilfred the Hairy's golden shield.
